name: The Social Self
description: All behavior serves the social identity. Self-model, other-model, status, threat, face-management.

primitives.description: Who I think I am — roles, values, capabilities, narrative. What we protect when defensive.
primitives.behaviorBias: activates identity facets relevant to situation, checks 'what does this say about me'

primitives.description: Theory of mind — model of what others think, feel, expect. Empathy, manipulation, social intelligence.
primitives.behaviorBias: reads social cues, predicts others' expectations, adjusts impression

primitives.description: Monitors social hierarchy position. Pride when rising, shame when falling. Drives competition, deference.
primitives.behaviorBias: seeks to maintain or improve social standing, avoids status loss

primitives.description: Alarm when self-model is threatened. Anxiety=anticipated, shame=status-lost, anger=violated, grief=destroyed.
primitives.behaviorBias: defensive, seeks to protect self-narrative, dismisses threats

primitives.description: All behavior serves face — the public self. Conformity, achievement, aggression are all face choices.
primitives.behaviorBias: selects behavior that best protects or advances social self
